content.full_text:
City of Alameda California

Oct 15 9 01 AM '92

General Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Letter of Legal Advice

Dear Sir/Madam:

Pursuant to Government Code section 83114 the City of
Alameda ("City") requests a letter of legal advice based upon the
facts described below. Specifically, the City requests advice on
the application of 2 Cal. Code of Regulations section 18702.3 to
staff members in the City's Community Development Department
("CDD"). Two staff members have interests in real property that
may conflict with their present duties in the CDD.

PROCEDURAL BACKGROUND

The CDD requested an opinion from the City Attorney's
office regarding application of section 18702.3 to staff members
handling residential rehabilitation projects. The Acting City
Attorney issued an opinion advising that designated employees with
a financial interest in real property that is within 300 feet of a
residential rehabilitation project should disqualify themselves.
(See attached memorandum.) Subsequently, the CDD asked the City
Attorney's office to request a letter of legal advice from the FPPC
in order to verify the City Attorney's opinion on this matter,
particularly as it relates to the differing responsibilities of
each of these employees.

FACTS

The CDD is responsible for administration and
implementation of various community development and economic
development projects in the City. Among these are a variety of
residential rehabilitation programs described below.

RESIDENTIAL REHABILITATION PROGRAMS

Substantial Rehabilitation Program ("SRP"): funds the addition of
new residential rental units to existing privately-owned single

Office of the City Attorney, Room 314

City Hall
2263 Santa Clara Avenue · 94501-4456
510.748.4544


General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
October 13, 1992
Page 2

family homes.     Subsequent occupancy is by Section 8 tenants
certified by the Alameda Housing Authority. Reduced interest loans
may be provided in amounts up to $80,000; additional construction
financing may be provided in excess of $100,000.

Rental Rehabilitation Program ("RRP"): provides deferred interest-
free loans on a dollar-for-dollar matching basis with owner's
private funding to correct deficiencies in existing residential
rental units. To be accepted, at least 70% of the units in a
complex must be occupied by low income persons at affordable rents
following rehabilitation. Loans may be a maximum of $60,000.

Housing Rehabilitation ("HR"): provides reduced interest loans to
low income homeowners for major rehabilitation projects, often
arising from years of deferred maintenance. Loans may be a maximum
of $30,000, from a variety of public and private sources.

Single Purpose Rehabilitation: various "minor" repair programs,
such as emergency grants and mini-loans, weatherization, and
utility undergrounding, are provided to low income homeowners.
Grants and loans range from $500 to $3,000.

CDD STAFF

     Two CDD staff members who handle residential rehabilitation
projects have indicated on financial disclosure statements that
they have interests in real property located in the City. A
question has arisen as to whether these staff members must
disqualify themselves from participation in residential
rehabilitation projects in the vicinity of their real property
interests.

     Outlined below is a description of each staff member's
job responsibilities and real property interests.

Carol Beaver, Neighborhood Development Manager

1.     Job Description

     The Neighborhood Development Manager ("NDM") supervises all
staff who implement residential rehabilitation programs. She does
no field work or inspections, generates no estimates on projects
costs and operating income (if any), does not serve on loan panels
which approve assistance, and does not initiate disbursement
requests.     Ms. Beaver does review data generated by staff to
determine whether assumptions are based on industry standards and

General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
October 13, 1992
Page 3


are consistent with prior practice and CDD policy.  The NDM has
primary responsibility for rehabilitation and program staff
supervision and accomplishment of program objectives within
existing regulatory and policy framework.

Single purpose rehabilitation programs are implemented through
subcontracts with other City departments or Alameda County Housing
and Community Development.  In these instances, the staff of the
subcontractor has primary responsibility similar to that of the
Reconstruction Specialist (described below), with project oversight
provided by CDD staff (either Reconstruction or Program
Specialists) who are, in turn, supervised by the NDM.  (At present,
Program Specialists are not required to disclose real property
interests.)

2.  Real Property Interest(s)

     a.  Ms. Beaver co-owns a residence in the City with a second
person.  This is the primary residence of both.

     b.  Ms. Beaver's co-owner in a. (above) also owns rental
property elsewhere in the City.

     c.  Ms. Beaver holds a second deed of trust on one
residential rental property in the City, owned 55% by her co-owner
in a. (above) with the remainder owned by a third party.

3.  Issues Raised

     a.  Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of
residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the
real property in which she has a direct interest (a. and c. above)?

     b.  Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of
residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the
real property held by her co-owner, in which she has no interest
(b. above) beyond their joint ownership of the primary residence?

     c.  For purposes of a conflict of interest determination
under section 18702.3, does it matter whether Ms. Beaver's real
property interest is her primary residence (a. above) rather than
income generating property (c. above)?

     d.  Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation she
supervises involves financial assistance for maintenance work
instead of addition of units?

General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
October 13, 1992
Page 4


     e.     Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation
projects she supervises are owner-occupied or rental property?

     f.     May projects from which Ms. Beaver must disqualify
herself from routine supervision be included on status reports
provided by her staff so that she may be aware of their full work
load?

Peter Ekstein, Reconstruction Specialist II

1.     Job Description

     Mr. Ekstein, like the other CDD Reconstruction
Specialist, goes to the project site to conduct inspections,
converses with owners and design professionals (if any), generates
all primary data regarding the nature, scope and cost of the
project, prepares loan panel data, and initiates disbursements.
Reconstruction Specialists function as project managers and have
primary responsibility for each phase of the project from estimate
through design and construction.  Mr. Ekstein has primary
responsibility for the SRP and initiates loan panels which
determine if owners receive SRP assistance.

2.     Real Property Interest(s)

     Mr. Ekstein owns rental units in the City.  He does not
reside in any of these units.

3.     Issues Raised

     a.     Must Mr. Ekstein disqualify himself from implementation
of SRP projects located within 300 feet of his real property
interest above?

     b.     For purposes of a conflict of interest determination
under section 18702.3, would it matter if Mr. Ekstein's real
property interest were his primary residence rather than income
generating property?

     c.     Would it matter if the residential rehabilitation
projects he handles received financial assistance for maintenance
work instead of addition of units?

     d.     For purposes of a potential conflict per Mr. Ekstein's
implementation, does it matter if the property owner resides in the

General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
October 13, 1992
Page 5


existing single family home which is receiving SRP assistance for
the addition of a rental unit?

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter.  If you
have any questions or need additional information, please contact
me at (510)748-4544.

                                        Very Truly Yours,


                                        [signature]
                                        Heather C. McLaughlin
                                        Assistant City Attorney

HCM:jo
Attachment

cc: City Attorney
    Community Development Director
    Program Specialist II

sections.question:
1. City of Alameda requests advice on the application of 2 Cal. Code of Regulations section 18702.3 to staff members in the City's Community Development Department who have interests in real property that may conflict with their present duties. Specifically:

For Carol Beaver (Neighborhood Development Manager):
a. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property in which she has a direct interest?
b. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property held by her co-owner, in which she has no interest beyond their joint ownership of the primary residence?
c. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, does it matter whether Ms. Beaver's real property interest is her primary residence rather than income generating property?
d. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation she supervises involves financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
e. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation projects she supervises are owner-occupied or rental property?
f. May projects from which Ms. Beaver must disqualify herself from routine supervision be included on status reports provided by her staff so that she may be aware of their full work load?

For Peter Ekstein (Reconstruction Specialist):
a. Must Mr. Ekstein disqualify himself from implementation of SRP projects located within 300 feet of his real property interest?
b. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, would it matter if Mr. Ekstein's real property interest were his primary residence rather than income generating property?
c. Would it matter if the residential rehabilitation projects he handles received financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
d. For purposes of a potential conflict per Mr. Ekstein's implementation, does it matter if the property owner resides in the existing single family home which is receiving SRP assistance for the addition of a rental unit?

sections.question_synthetic: The City of Alameda requests FPPC guidance on whether two Community Development Department staff members must disqualify themselves from participating in residential rehabilitation projects within 300 feet of their real property interests under California Code of Regulations section 18702.3, and clarification on various factors that may affect conflict of interest determinations.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the City of Alameda requesting FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains detailed factual background about two employees' job responsibilities and real property interests, followed by specific questions posed to the FPPC. No formal CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present because this is the request, not the response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. City of Alameda requests advice on the application of 2 Cal. Code of Regulations section 18702.3 to staff members in the City's Community Development Department who have interests in real property that may conflict with their present duties. Specifically:

For Carol Beaver (Neighborhood Development Manager):
a. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property in which she has a direct interest?
b. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property held by her co-owner, in which she has no interest beyond their joint ownership of the primary residence?
c. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, does it matter whether Ms. Beaver's real property interest is her primary residence rather than income generating property?
d. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation she supervises involves financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
e. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation projects she supervises are owner-occupied or rental property?
f. May projects from which Ms. Beaver must disqualify herself from routine supervision be included on status reports provided by her staff so that she may be aware of their full work load?

For Peter Ekstein (Reconstruction Specialist):
a. Must Mr. Ekstein disqualify himself from implementation of SRP projects located within 300 feet of his real property interest?
b. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, would it matter if Mr. Ekstein's real property interest were his primary residence rather than income generating property?
c. Would it matter if the residential rehabilitation projects he handles received financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
d. For purposes of a potential conflict per Mr. Ekstein's implementation, does it matter if the property owner resides in the existing single family home which is receiving SRP assistance for the addition of a rental unit?

embedding.summary: The City of Alameda requests a letter of legal advice from the FPPC regarding whether two Community Development Department staff members (Carol Beaver, Neighborhood Development Manager, and Peter Ekstein, Reconstruction Specialist) must disqualify themselves from residential rehabilitation projects near their real property interests under section 18702.3.
